extraction.one_line_summary: VLASOV Alexander won from a five-rider sprint after LECHNER and FUGLSANG split the GC group on the penultimate climb, with EVENEPOEL dropped twice.
tactical_patterns: Israel Premier Tech isolated opponents by splitting race on penultimate climb 4km out, Fuglsang attacking twice before cooperation.
INEOS controlled after Jumbo-Visma withdrew entirely due to COVID, suggesting immediate tactical vacancy in stage race peloton control.
Five-rider selection coalesced through attacks, then effectively neutralized to chat before sprinting, revealing conservative GC risk management.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: dropped twice on short climbs in 36-degree heat, showing first weakness after earlier struggle
rider_signals.FUGLSANG Jakob: positive: strong enough to attack twice in final 4km and still contest sprint, better form than expected
rider_signals.VLASOV Alexander: positive: won sprint and took yellow after Tour de Romandie win, suggesting peaking trajectory toward July
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: desperately trying to hang on before key climbs, indicating poor heat tolerance or fading form
rider_signals.YATES Adam: negative: COVID withdrawal eliminated INEOS GC leadership before mountain stages
